subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.1.1.1. Demonstrate skills in dealing with environmental challenges
indicator_description: Examine air pollution as an environmental challenge
examplars:
1. Explain what air pollution means. [cite: 2179]
2. Identify activities in the environment that lead to air pollution, including fumes from vehicles, industry and smoke from fires. [cite: 2179]
3. Discuss the effects of air pollution, including causing health problems such as asthma. [cite: 2185]
4. Discuss ways to prevent air pollution. [cite: 2188]
5. Do a project in the community to educate members on the ways to reduce air pollution. [cite: 2190]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.1.1.2. Evaluate the sources and ways of conserving energy
indicator_description: Assess global sources and the means of conserving energy
examplars:
1. Identify global energy sources and conservation in countries such as Ghana, United Kingdom, Germany. [cite: 2193]
2. With the use of the Internet, research alternative energy sources and do a presentation in class. [cite: 2196]
3. Discuss gender issues related to energy production, supply and usage. [cite: 2198]
4. Describe the processes and devices for measuring and metering energy consumption. [cite: 2202]
5. In groups, create posters on the advantages of energy conservation to educate others in the community. [cite: 2204]
6. Present a report on energy conservation education that you have undertaken. [cite: 2207]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.1.4.1. Investigate the natural and human resources around us
indicator_description: Examine the importance of natural resources to the development of Ghana
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by natural resources. [cite: 2213]
2. Through the use of videos or pictures, identify the natural resources of the country, including renewable and non-renewable resources. [cite: 2217]
3. On the map of Ghana, show the location of the natural resources. [cite: 2219]
4. Discuss efficient ways of exploiting natural resources. [cite: 2220]
5. Explain the importance of natural resources for the development of the country. [cite: 2225]
6. Discuss the difficulty in exploiting natural resources effectively. [cite: 2229]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.1.4.1. Investigate the natural and human resources around us
indicator_description: Evaluate the importance of human resources to the development of Ghana
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by human resource. [cite: 2233]
2. Identify ways of developing human resource. [cite: 2238]
3. Through group work, discuss why an educated and trained human resource is the most important asset of the country. [cite: 2239]
4. Explain how Ghana’s human resource can be efficiently used in the production of goods and services. [cite: 2242]
5. Investigate how some people in the community use their human resource abilities to help the community develop. [cite: 2246]
6. Suggest new ways by which they would use their human resource abilities to help develop the community. [cite: 2250]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.2.2.1. Evaluate the institution of marriage in Ghana
indicator_description: Examine the importance of marriage as a social institution in Ghana
examplars:
1. Explain the concept of marriage as a social institution. [cite: 2255]
2. Discuss ways of contracting marriages in Ghana. [cite: 2257]
3. Identify the ways in which marriage is important as a social institution. [cite: 2259]
4. Investigate the causes of broken marriages. [cite: 2266]
5. Dramatise the effects of broken marriages on the family structure. [cite: 2270]
6. Suggest ways of sustaining marriages. [cite: 2275]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.2.2.2. Assess the need for responsible parenting in the family
indicator_description: Examine the importance of responsible parenting within the family system
examplars:
1. Explain the concepts: Parenthood, Parenting. [cite: 2279]
2. Differentiate parenthood from parenting. [cite: 2282]
3. Discuss the responsibilities of parents. [cite: 2284]
4. Discuss inappropriate parenting behaviour and its consequences. [cite: 2287]
5. Examine how some behaviours by children can cause their parents to act inappropriately. [cite: 2293]
6. Suggest ways for handling parent-child conflicts. [cite: 2296]
7. Carry out an education programme in the community on the need for parents to be responsible. [cite: 2299]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.2.4.1. Analyse the population structure in Ghana and its related issues
indicator_description: Assess population structure in Ghana and its associated development issues
examplars:
1. Recap on Basic 8 work on the population structure in Ghana. [cite: 2307]
2. In groups, analyse the effects of rapid population growth on the family, the community and the nation. [cite: 2311]
3. Dramatise the effects of large family size on the upkeep of the family. [cite: 2320]
4. Suggest ways in which social problems and rapid population growth could be minimised. [cite: 2325]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.3.3.1. Evaluate the place of culture in national identity
indicator_description: Assess the significance of symbols, music and ceremonies in promoting national identity
examplars:
1. Explain the concept of national identity. [cite: 2332]
2. Sing patriotic songs and discuss how songs promote national identity. [cite: 2335]
3. With the use of videos or pictures, identify some national and traditional symbols and discuss their features and significance. [cite: 2337]
4. Interpret some national and traditional symbols and evaluate their importance in fostering national unity. [cite: 2342]
5. Discuss ways of fostering national unity and defending the integrity of the nation. [cite: 2344]
6. Discuss how national ceremonies can be used to foster national unity. [cite: 2347]
7. Discuss how respecting the customs and culture of other groups plays a part in celebrating diversity in Ghana. [cite: 2350]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.4.3.1. Assess the relevance of the 1992 Constitution
indicator_description: Examine the 1992 Constitution and its significance to national development
examplars:
1. Explain the meaning of a constitution. [cite: 2359]
2. Outline the main features of the 1992 Constitution. [cite: 2362]
3. Use videos or pictures to identify the functions of the three main organs of government. [cite: 2363]
4. In groups, discuss the rights, freedoms and obligations of citizens under the 1992 Constitution. [cite: 2368]
5. Examine the conditions under which the rights of a citizen can be curtailed. [cite: 2375]
6. Explain the importance of the 1992 Constitution to Ghanaians. [cite: 2379]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.4.3.1. Assess the relevance of the 1992 Constitution
indicator_description: Discuss the rights of the child as enshrined in the 1992 Constitution
examplars:
1. Explain the concepts of child rights. [cite: 2387]
2. Examine the provisions of child rights in the 1992 Constitution. [cite: 2390]
3. Invite a resource person to discuss the need for children’s rights. [cite: 2393]
4. Carry out a project on the responsibilities of children. [cite: 2399]
5. Explain the concept of child abuse. [cite: 2404]
6. Identify acts that constitute child abuse, including child labour and early marriages. [cite: 2407]
7. Examine the effects of child abuse. [cite: 2409]

subject: Social Studies
sub_strand: PEACE AND SECURITY IN OUR NATION
content_standard: B9.4.4.1. Assess the role of peace and security in national development
indicator_description: Examine the relevance of peace and security in our community
examplars:
1. Explain the concepts of peace and security. [cite: 2415]
2. Discuss ways of promoting peace and security. [cite: 2418]
3. With the use of video and pictures, compare a country destroyed by conflict and another that has enjoyed peace. [cite: 2421]
4. Using documentaries and pictures, examine safety measures in the community. [cite: 2426]
5. Discuss the importance of peace and security in community development. [cite: 2430]
6. Role play how peace can be maintained in your neighbourhood. [cite: 2434]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.4.5.1. Show understanding of how to promote democracy and political stability
indicator_description: Examine election as a way of promoting democracy and political stability
examplars:
1. Explain the concepts of democracy and election. [cite: 2441]
2. Examine the role of free and fair elections in promoting democracy. [cite: 2443]
3. Discuss the processes involved in conducting an election. [cite: 2448]
4. Identify and discuss the importance of electoral materials. [cite: 2452]
5. Watch videos of people casting their votes peacefully and discuss. [cite: 2455]
6. Demonstrate how free and fair elections can be conducted using role-play. [cite: 2460]
7. Assess the role of the Electoral Commission. [cite: 2465]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.4.5.2. Demonstrate understanding of the District Assembly concept in Ghana
indicator_description: Examine the role of the District Assembly in promoting decentralisation
examplars:
1. Explain the concept of decentralisation. [cite: 2471]
2. Describe the structure of the District Assembly as a decentralised arm of government. [cite: 2473]
3. Examine the role of District Assemblies in the development of the districts. [cite: 2477]
4. Examine the contribution of the Assemblies, including Metropolitan, Municipal, District. [cite: 2483]
5. Invite a resource person to talk about the workings of the MMDCs. [cite: 2486]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.4.5.3. Evaluate the importance of political stability in Ghana’s development
indicator_description: Assess the significance of political stability in national development
examplars:
1. Explain political stability. [cite: 2489]
2. Examine the ways of achieving political stability. [cite: 2493]
3. Through the use of videos and pictures, discuss some of the threats to political stability. [cite: 2496]
4. Discuss the importance of political stability. [cite: 2499]
5. Suggest ways of promoting political stability in Ghana. [cite: 2504]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.5.4.1. Analyse the contribution of science and technology to national development
indicator_description: Examine how science and technology can be used to promote development
examplars:
1. Explain the concepts: Science, Technology. [cite: 2511]
2. Examine the difference(s) between ‘Science’ and ‘Technology’. [cite: 2514]
3. Discuss how science and technology can be used for development and production in Ghana. [cite: 2516]
4. Examine the role of science and technology in specific sectors of the economy. [cite: 2522]
5. With the use of the Internet investigate the role of science and technology in development. [cite: 2525]
6. Examine the problems impeding the development of science and technology in Ghana. [cite: 2528]
7. Suggest ways of promoting science and technology in Ghana. [cite: 2533]
8. Project: Identify a problem in the school and use science and technology to solve it. [cite: 2537]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.5.2.1. Show understanding of the provisions under National Pensions Act 766 and PNDC Law 247
indicator_description: Exhibit knowledge on pension rights under National Pensions Act 766 and PNDC Law 247
examplars:
1. Discuss the provisions under National Pensions Act 766. [cite: 2543]
2. In small groups, do a presentation on the provisions under PNDC Law 247. [cite: 2546]
3. Invite a resource person to talk about social security in Ghana. [cite: 2552]
4. In small groups, do a presentation on the benefits under the Three Tier Pension Scheme. [cite: 2555]
5. Research the application processes for the various SSNIT benefits. [cite: 2561]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.6.2.1. Demonstrate understanding that Ghana had two republics between 1979 and 2000
indicator_description: Explain how the Third Republic came into being (1979-1981)
examplars:
1. Use the internet, print media and oral sources to find out how the Third Republic was established. [cite: 2569]
2. Identify the main political parties that took part in the June 1979 elections. [cite: 2571]
3. Discuss the outcome of the June 1979 elections. [cite: 2573]
4. Dramatise the handing over of power from the AFRC to Dr Hilla Liman’s PNP. [cite: 2578]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.6.2.1. Demonstrate understanding that Ghana had two republics between 1979 and 2000
indicator_description: Explain political developments under the Third Republic
examplars:
1. Identify the main features of the Third Republican Constitution. [cite: 2581]
2. Discuss the impact of the Third Republican Constitution on political development. [cite: 2584]
3. Compare the features of the Second and Third Republican Constitution. [cite: 2588]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.6.2.1. Demonstrate understanding that Ghana had two republics between 1979 and 2000
indicator_description: Explain how the Fourth Republic came into being
examplars:
1. Use the Internet, print media and oral sources to find out how the Fourth Republic was established. [cite: 2591]
2. Discuss the outcome of the 28 April 1992 referendum. [cite: 2593]
3. Identify the political parties that took part in the 1992 elections. [cite: 2599]
4. Discuss the nature of the First Parliament of the Fourth Republic. [cite: 2602]
5. Dramatise the inauguration of the first president of the Fourth Republic. [cite: 2606]
6. Analyse the 1992 constitution vis-a-vis the American constitution and Westminster system. [cite: 2608]

subject: Social Studies
content_standard: B9.6.2.1. Demonstrate understanding that Ghana had two republics between 1979 and 2000
indicator_description: Explain political developments under the Fourth Republic
examplars:
1. Discuss the features of the Fourth Republican constitution. [cite: 2615]
2. Debate the pros and cons of the First Parliament of the Fourth Republic. [cite: 2617]
3. Assess the advantages of the President being Head of State and Commander in Chief. [cite: 2625]
4. Compare Ghana’s presidential system and the Westminster system. [cite: 2628]
